---
label: "MCP-Connected Agents"
icon: "🔗"
---

## Connecting Agents to the Real World via MCP

The power of MCP is that once you build an MCP server, **any** MCP-compatible agent can use it. Your supplier validation server works with Claude Desktop, with your custom LangGraph agent, with VS Code Copilot — all without changing a line of server code.

### Building a 3-Agent System with MCP

<CodeBlock b64="" />

### MCP Server Configuration for Claude Desktop

<CodeBlock b64="" />

<AnalogyBox emoji="🌐" title="API Gateway Pattern">
MCP configuration is like setting up an API gateway. Each server entry is a microservice that the AI agent can discover and call. The agent doesn't need to know implementation details — it just sees the tools, resources, and prompts each server exposes. This is exactly how enterprise API management works: register services → discover capabilities → call what you need.
</AnalogyBox>

<Quiz
  question="In a 3-agent system (Researcher → Analyst → Writer), the Writer produces a report that scores 5/10 on quality. What should happen?"
  options={["Restart from the Researcher", "Send back to the Writer for revision with the quality feedback", "End and return the low-quality report", "Replace the Writer with a different LLM model"]}
  correctIndex={1}
  explanation="Revision should go back to the Writer, not restart from scratch. The research and analysis are likely fine — the writing just needs improvement. This is the same principle as document revision in any organization: you don't redo the research when the draft needs editing."
/>

<SeeItInRe3 text="Re³'s debate pipeline is essentially a 3-agent system with quality gates: Forge (researcher/curator) → Debater agents (analysts) → Sage/Hypatia (writers/synthesizers), with Atlas acting as the quality gate between rounds." />
